sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.email_address FROM People AS T1 JOIN Students AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.student_id WHERE T1.person_id IN (SELECT T3.person_id FROM People_Addresses AS T3 JOIN Addresses AS T4 ON T3.address_id = T4.address_id WHERE T4.state_province_county = 'Texas');

sql: SELECT T3.first_name, T3.last_name FROM Student_Course_Attendance AS T1 JOIN Courses AS T2 ON T1.course_id = T2.course_id JOIN People AS T3 ON T1.student_id = T3.person_id WHERE T1.course_id = (SELECT course_id FROM Student_Course_Attendance GROUP BY course_id ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1);

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.supplier_name FROM Suppliers AS T1 JOIN Product_Suppliers AS T2 ON T1.supplier_id = T2.supplier_id JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.product_id = T3.product_id WHERE T3.product_price > (SELECT AVG(product_price) FROM Products);

nl_question: List asset IDs and their details for all assets currently under a maintenance contract but which have no recorded fault entries.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Maintenance_Engineers AS T1 JOIN Engineer_Visits AS T2 ON T1.engineer_id = T2.engineer_id JOIN Fault_Log AS T3 ON T2.fault_log_entry_id = T3.fault_log_entry_id JOIN Assets AS T4 ON T3.asset_id = T4.asset_id JOIN Asset_Parts AS T5 ON T4.asset_id = T5.asset_id JOIN Parts AS T6 ON T5.part_id = T6.part_id WHERE T6.part_name = 'package' AND T6.chargeable_yn = '1';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the unique first and last names of maintenance engineers who have worked on assets that used a chargeable part named 'package'.

sql: SELECT T1.forename, T1.surname, CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T2.statusId = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T2.resultId) AS finish_percentage FROM drivers AS T1 JOIN results AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId GROUP BY T1.driverId HAVING COUNT(T2.resultId) >= 50 ORDER BY finish_percentage DESC;
result_summary: Returns 149 rows listing the race finish percentage for drivers with at least 50 race entries, ordered from the highest percentage to the lowest.
schema:
Database: formula_1

Table: circuits

sql: SELECT STRFTIME('%Y-%m', datetime_payment) AS payment_month, SUM(amount_payment) AS total_payments FROM Customer_Payments GROUP BY payment_month ORDER BY total_payments DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single month with the highest total customer payments, showing the month and the corresponding total payment amount.

sql: SELECT T1.CustomerId FROM Customer AS T1 JOIN Invoice AS T2 ON T1.CustomerId = T2.CustomerId JOIN InvoiceLine AS T3 ON T2.InvoiceId = T3.InvoiceId JOIN Track AS T4 ON T3.TrackId = T4.TrackId JOIN Album AS T5 ON T4.AlbumId = T5.AlbumId JOIN Artist AS T6 ON T5.ArtistId = T6.ArtistId WHERE T6.Name = 'AC/DC' EXCEPT SELECT T1.CustomerId FROM Customer AS T1 JOIN Invoice AS T2 ON T1.CustomerId = T2.CustomerId JOIN InvoiceLine AS T3 ON T2.InvoiceId = T3.InvoiceId JOIN Track AS T4 ON T3.TrackId = T4.TrackId JOIN Album AS T5 ON T4.AlbumId = T5.AlbumId JOIN Artist AS T6 ON T5.ArtistId = T6.ArtistId WHERE T6.Name = 'Accept';

sql: SELECT T1.forename, T1.surname, SUM(T2.points) AS total_points FROM drivers AS T1 JOIN results AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId GROUP BY T1.driverId HAVING SUM(T2.points) > (SELECT AVG(T4.points) FROM drivers AS T3 JOIN results AS T4 ON T3.driverId = T4.driverId WHERE T3.nationality = 'German');

sql: SELECT T1.year, T3.forename, T3.surname, T2.wins FROM races AS T1 JOIN driverStandings AS T2 ON T1.raceId = T2.raceId JOIN drivers AS T3 ON T2.driverId = T3.driverId JOIN (SELECT T4.year, MAX(T5.wins) AS max_wins FROM races AS T4 JOIN driverStandings AS T5 ON T4.raceId = T5.raceId WHERE T5.wins > 0 GROUP BY T4.year) AS T6 ON T1.year = T6.year AND T2.wins = T6.max_wins GROUP BY T1.year;
result_summary: Returns 68 rows, identifying one driver with the most race wins for each year from 1950 onwards, along with their full name and total win count for that season.
schema:
Database: formula_1

sql: SELECT T1.forename, T1.surname, COUNT(T2.raceId) AS race_count, SUM(T2.points) AS career_points FROM drivers AS T1 JOIN results AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId GROUP BY T1.driverId HAVING COUNT(T2.raceId) > 150 ORDER BY career_points DESC;
result_summary: Returns 37 rows listing experienced drivers who have competed in over 150 races, showing their total race count and career points, ordered from the highest total career points to the lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.nationality, SUM(T2.laps) AS total_laps FROM drivers AS T1 JOIN results AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId GROUP BY T1.nationality ORDER BY total_laps DESC;
result_summary: Returns 41 rows listing each driver nationality along with the total number of race laps completed by drivers from that country, ordered from highest to lowest total laps.

sql: SELECT T4.forename, T4.surname, T3.name, T2.position AS qualifying_pos, T1.positionOrder AS race_pos, (T2.position - T1.positionOrder) AS positions_gained FROM results AS T1 JOIN qualifying AS T2 ON T1.raceId = T2.raceId AND T1.driverId = T2.driverId JOIN races AS T3 ON T1.raceId = T3.raceId JOIN drivers AS T4 ON T1.driverId = T4.driverId JOIN status AS T5 ON T1.statusId = T5.statusId WHERE T5.status = 'Finished' ORDER BY positions_gained DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the driver who gained the most positions from their qualifying start to their final finish in a single completed race, showing their name, the race details, and the number of positions gained.

sql: SELECT T1.company_name, T2.FaultCount FROM Third_Party_Companies AS T1 JOIN (SELECT T3.company_id, COUNT(T5.fault_log_entry_id) AS FaultCount FROM Maintenance_Engineers AS T3 JOIN Engineer_Visits AS T4 ON T3.engineer_id = T4.engineer_id JOIN Fault_Log AS T5 ON T4.fault_log_entry_id = T5.fault_log_entry_id GROUP BY T3.company_id) AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.company_id WHERE T2.FaultCount > 0;

sql: SELECT T4.name, T2.forename, T2.surname, T3.forename, T3.surname FROM results AS T1_A JOIN results AS T1_B ON T1_A.raceId = T1_B.raceId AND T1_A.constructorId = T1_B.constructorId AND T1_A.driverId < T1_B.driverId JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T1_A.driverId = T2.driverId JOIN drivers AS T3 ON T1_B.driverId = T3.driverId JOIN races AS T4 ON T1_A.raceId = T4.raceId WHERE T4.name = 'Australian Grand Prix';

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.forename, T1.surname, T5.name FROM drivers AS T1 JOIN results AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId JOIN qualifying AS T3 ON T1.driverId = T3.driverId AND T2.raceId = T3.raceId JOIN lapTimes AS T4 ON T1.driverId = T4.driverId AND T2.raceId = T4.raceId JOIN races AS T5 ON T2.raceId = T5.raceId WHERE T2.positionOrder = 1 AND T3.position = 1 AND T2.rank = 1 AND T4.lap = 1 AND T4.position = 1;
result_summary: Returns 36 rows listing the drivers and the specific races where they achieved a dominant victory, defined as starting on pole, leading after the first lap, setting the fastest lap, and winning the race.
schema:
Database: formula_1

Table: circuits

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, SUM(T4.product_price) AS total_spent FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id ORDER BY total_spent DESC LIMIT 3;

sql: SELECT T1.order_id, T1.order_date, T2.item_count FROM Customer_Orders AS T1 JOIN (SELECT order_id, COUNT(*) AS item_count FROM Order_Items GROUP BY order_id) AS T2 ON T1.order_id = T2.order_id ORDER BY T2.item_count DESC LIMIT 1;

sql: SELECT T1.asset_id, T1.asset_details FROM Assets AS T1 JOIN Fault_Log AS T2 ON T1.asset_id = T2.asset_id JOIN Fault_Log_Parts AS T3 ON T2.fault_log_entry_id = T3.fault_log_entry_id JOIN Skills_Required_To_Fix AS T4 ON T3.part_fault_id = T4.part_fault_id JOIN Skills AS T5 ON T4.skill_id = T5.skill_id WHERE T5.skill_description IN ('Electrical', 'Mechanical') GROUP BY T1.asset_id, T1.asset_details HAVING count(DISTINCT T5.skill_id) = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the asset ID and details for assets that have had faults requiring both 'Electrical' and 'Mechanical' skills for their repair.

result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the entry ID and description for 'PW' type part faults that were handled by the maintenance company 'Schuppe-Carroll'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T3.TrackId) AS RockTrackCount FROM Artist AS T1 JOIN Album AS T2 ON T1.ArtistId = T2.ArtistId JOIN Track AS T3 ON T2.AlbumId = T3.AlbumId JOIN Genre AS T4 ON T3.GenreId = T4.GenreId WHERE T4.Name = 'Rock' GROUP BY T1.ArtistId ORDER BY RockTrackCount DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single artist with the most rock tracks in the catalog, showing their name and the total count.

sql: SELECT T2.year, AVG(T1.num_stops) AS avg_stops FROM (SELECT raceId, COUNT(stop) AS num_stops FROM pitStops GROUP BY raceId) AS T1 JOIN races AS T2 ON T1.raceId = T2.raceId GROUP BY T2.year ORDER BY avg_stops DESC LIMIT 1;

result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique skills required to fix faults on assets provided by the supplier with company ID 2.

sql: WITH CountryGenreSales AS (SELECT I.BillingCountry, G.Name AS GenreName, SUM(IL.UnitPrice * IL.Quantity) AS TotalSales FROM Invoice AS I JOIN InvoiceLine AS IL ON I.InvoiceId = IL.InvoiceId JOIN Track AS T ON IL.TrackId = T.TrackId JOIN Genre AS G ON T.GenreId = G.GenreId GROUP BY I.BillingCountry, G.Name) SELECT T1.BillingCountry, T1.GenreName, T1.TotalSales FROM CountryGenreSales AS T1 WHERE T1.TotalSales = (SELECT MAX(T2.TotalSales) FROM CountryGenreSales AS T2 WHERE T2.BillingCountry = T1.BillingCountry) ORDER BY T1.BillingCountry;
result_summary: Returns 25 rows identifying the top-selling music genre(s) for each country, along with the corresponding total sales, ordered alphabetically by country.
